questions.question:
The set of all positive integers is the union of two disjoint subsets \( \{f(1), f(2),\dots ,f(n),\dots\} \) and \( \{g(1), g(2),\dots,g(n),\dots\}, \) where \( f(1) < f(2) <\dots < f(n) < \dots, \) and \( g(1) < g(2) <\dots< g(n) < \dots, \) and \( g(n) = f(f(n)) + 1 \) for all \( n \geq 1. \) What is the value of \( g(1)? \)



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 2000 | Question: 105

For all non-negative integers \( x \) and \( y, f(x, y) \) is defined as below

  • \( f(0, y) = y + 1 \)
  • \( f(x + 1, 0) = f(x, 1) \)
  • \( f(x + 1,y + 1) = f(x, f(x + 1, y)) \)

Then, what is the value of \( f(1, 2)? \)



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 2000 | Question: 107

Answer the following question based on the information given below.
For real numbers \( x, y, \) let

\( f(x, y) = \left\{\begin{matrix} \text{Positive square-root of}\; (x + y),\;\text{if}\; (x + y)^{0.5}\;\text{is real} \\  (x + y)^2,\;\text{otherwise} \end{matrix}\right. \)
\( g(x, y) = \left\{\begin{matrix} (x + y)^2,\;\text{if}\; (x + y)^{0.5}\;\text{is real} \\  –(x + y),\;\text{otherwise} \end{matrix}\right. \)

Which of the following expressions yields a positive value for every pair of non-zero real number \( (x, y)? \)



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 2000 | Question: 75

Answer the following question based on the information given below.
For real numbers \( x, y, \) let

\( f(x, y) = \left\{\begin{matrix} \text{Positive square-root of}\; (x + y),\;\text{if}\; (x + y)^{0.5}\;\text{is real} \\  (x + y)^2,\;\text{otherwise} \end{matrix}\right. \)
\( g(x, y) = \left\{\begin{matrix} (x + y)^2,\;\text{if}\; (x + y)^{0.5}\;\text{is real} \\  –(x + y),\;\text{otherwise} \end{matrix}\right. \)

Under which of the following conditions is \( f(x, y) \) necessarily greater than \( g(x, y)? \)



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 2000 | Question: 76
options: Both \( x \) and \( y \) are less than \( –1 \)

questions.question:
Answer the following question based on the information given below.
For three distinct real numbers \( x, y \) and \( z, \) let

  • \( f(x, y, z) = \min(\max(x, y), \max(y, z), \max(z, x)) \)
  • \( g(x, y, z) = \max(\min(x, y), \min(y, z), \min(z, x)) \)
  • \( h(x, y, z) = \max(\max(x, y), \max(y, z), \max(z, x)) \)
  • \( j(x, y, z) = \min(\min(x, y), \min(y, z), \min(z, x)) \)
  • \( m(x, y, z) = \max(x, y, z) \)
  • \( n(x, y, z) = \min(x, y, z) \)

Which of the following is necessarily greater than \( 1? \)



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 2000 | Question: 77

Answer the following question based on the information given below.
For three distinct real numbers \( x, y \) and \( z, \) let

  • \( f(x, y, z) = \min(\max(x, y), \max(y, z), \max(z, x)) \)
  • \( g(x, y, z) = \max(\min(x, y), \min(y, z), \min(z, x)) \)
  • \( h(x, y, z) = \max(\max(x, y), \max(y, z), \max(z, x)) \)
  • \( j(x, y, z) = \min(\min(x, y), \min(y, z), \min(z, x)) \)
  • \( m(x, y, z) = \max(x, y, z) \)
  • \( n(x, y, z) = \min(x, y, z) \)

Which of the following expressions is necessarily equal to \( 1? \)



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 2000 | Question: 78

Answer the following question based on the information given below.
For three distinct real numbers \( x, y \) and \( z, \) let

  • \( f(x, y, z) = \min(\max(x, y), \max(y, z), \max(z, x)) \)
  • \( g(x, y, z) = \max(\min(x, y), \min(y, z), \min(z, x)) \)
  • \( h(x, y, z) = \max(\max(x, y), \max(y, z), \max(z, x)) \)
  • \( j(x, y, z) = \min(\min(x, y), \min(y, z), \min(z, x)) \)
  • \( m(x, y, z) = \max(x, y, z) \)
  • \( n(x, y, z) = \min(x, y, z) \)

Which of the following expressions is indeterminate?



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 2000 | Question: 79

Answer the following question based on the information given below.
Given below is a graph made up of straight-line segments shown as thick lines. Choose the answer as

  1. if \( f(x) = 3 f(–x); \)
  2. if \( f(x) = –f(–x); \)
  3. if \( f(x) = f(–x); \) and
  4. if \( 3 f(x) = 6 f(–x), \) for \( x\geq 0. \)

Given below is a graph made up of straight-line segments shown as thick lines. Choose the answer as

  3. if \( f(x) = f(–x); \) and

Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 2000 | Question: 84

Answer the following question based on the information given below.
For a real number \( x \), let

f(x)

= 1/(1 + x),

if x is non-negative
 

= 1+ x,

if x is negative

f\( ^n \)(x)

= f(f\( ^{n – 1} \)(x)), n = 2, 3, ....

  

What is the value of the product, \( f(2)f^2(2)f^3(2)f^4(2)f^5(2) \)?



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 2000 | Question: 87

Answer the following question based on the information given below.
For a real number \( x, \) let

f(x)

= 1/(1 + x),

if x is non-negative
 

= 1+ x,

if x is negative

f\( ^n \)(x)

= f(f\( ^{n – 1} \)(x)), n = 2, 3, ....

  

\( r \) is an integer \( \geq 2. \) Then what is the value of \( f^{r-1}(-r) + f^r(-r)+f^{r+1}(-r) \)?

Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 2003 | Question: 1

If \( f(x) = x^3 - 4x + p \), and \( f(0) \) and \( f(1) \) are of opposite signs, then which of the following is necessarily true?



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 2004 | Question: 47

Answer the questions on the basis of the information given below:

  • \( f_{1}(x) = \left\{\begin{matrix} x & 0 \leq x \leq 1 \\  1 & x \geq 1 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{matrix}\right. \)  
  • \( f_{2}(x) =  f_{1}(-x) \;\; \text{for all} \; x \)
  • \( f_{3}(x) =  -f_{2}(-x) \;\; \text{for all} \; x \)
  • \( f_{4}(x) =  f_{3}(-x) \;\; \text{for all} \; x \)

 How many of the following products are necessarily zero for every \( x: f_1(x)f_2(x), \: f_2(x)f_3(x), \: f_2(x)f_4(x) \)



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 2004 | Question: 61

Answer the questions on the basis of the information given below:

  • \( f_{1}(x) = \left\{\begin{matrix} x & 0 \leq x \leq 1 \\  1 & x \geq 1 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{matrix}\right. \)  
  • \( f_{2}(x) =  f_{1}(-x) \;\; \text{for all} \; x \)
  • \( f_{3}(x) =  -f_{2}(-x) \;\; \text{for all} \; x \)
  • \( f_{4}(x) =  f_{3}(-x) \;\; \text{for all} \; x \)

 Which of the following is necessarily true?



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 2004 | Question: 62

Let \( g(x) \) be a function such that \( g(x+1) +g(x-1) = g(x) \) for every real \( x. \) Then for what value of \( p \) is the relation \( g(x+p)=g(x) \) necessarily true for every real \( x? \)



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 2005 | Question: 27

A function \( f(x) \) satisfies \( f(1)=3600 \) and \( f(1)+f(2)+ \dots + f(n) = n^2 f(n) \), for all positive integers \( n>1. \) What is the value of \( f(9)? \)

Let \( f(x) \) be a function satisfying \( f(x) f(y) =f(xy) \) for all real \( x, y \). If \( f(2)=4 \), then what is the value of \( f\left(\frac{1}{2}\right) \)

If \( g(x)=p\mid x \mid-qx^2 \), where \( p \) and \( q \) are constants, then at \( x=0, g(x) \) will be



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 2013 | Question: 23

Let \( f(x)= \dfrac{1}{1+x^2} \) and \( g(x)=\dfrac{e^{−x}}{1+[x]} \), where \( [x] \) is the greatest integer less than or equal to \( x \). Then which of the following domain is true?

  1. domain of \( (f+g)=R-(-2,-1] \)
  2. domain of \( (f+g)=R-[-1,0) \)
  3. \( [\text{range of f}] \cap [\text{range of g}]=\bigg[-2, \dfrac{1}{2} \bigg] \)
  4. \( [\text{range of f}] \cap [\text{range of g}]= \bigg[- \dfrac{1}{2},\dfrac{1}{2} \bigg]-\{0\} \)


Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 2013 | Question: 29

If \( y = f(x) \) and \( f(x) = (1 - x) / (1 + x) \), which of the following is true? 

  1. \( f(2x) = f(x) – 1 \) 
  2. \( x = f(2y) - 1 \) 
  3. \( f(1/x) = f(x) \) 
  4. \( x = f(y) \)


Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 2014 | Question: 11

Use the following information for next two questions: A function \( f(x) \) is said to be even if \( f(-x) = f(x) \), and odd if \( f(-x) = -f(x) \). Thus, for example, the function given by \( f(x)=x^{2} \) is even, while the function given by \( f(x)=x^{3} \) is odd. Using this definition, answer the following questions.

The function given by \( f(x) = |x|^{3} \)

 A function \( f(x) \) is said to be even if \( f(-x) = f(x) \), and odd if \( f(-x) = -f(x) \). Thus, for example, the function given by \( f(x)=x^{2} \) is even, while the function given by \( f(x)=x^{3} \) is odd. Using this definition, answer the following questions.

The sum of two odd functions 



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 2014 | Question: 33

A quadratic function \( ƒ(x) \) attains a maximum of \( 3 \) at \( x = 1 \). The value of the function at \( x = 0 \) is \( 1 \). What is the value \( ƒ(x) \) at \( x = 10 \)? 

  1. \( –119 \) 
  2. \( –159 \) 
  3. \( –110 \) 
  4. \( –180 \)


Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 2014 | Question: 50

The set of all positive integers is the union of two disjoint subsets\( :\left \{ f\left ( 1 \right ),f\left ( 2 \right ), \dots, f\left ( n \right ), \dots \right \} \) and \( \left \{ g\left ( 1 \right ),g\left ( 2 \right ), \dots, g\left ( n \right ), \dots \right \} \), where \( f\left ( 1 \right )< f\left ( 2 \right )< \dots < f \left ( n \right ) \dots, \) and \( g\left ( 1 \right )< g \left ( 2 \right )< \dots < g\left ( n \right ) \dots, \) and\( g\left ( n \right )= f\left ( f\left ( n \right ) \right )+1 \) for all \( n \geq 1 \). What is the value of \( g\left ( 1 \right )? \)

  • \( f( 0,y) = y+1 \)
  • \( f(x+1,0) = f( x, 1) \)
  • \( f( x+1, y+1) = f\left( x,f( x+1,y) \right) \)

Then what is the value of \( f(1,2) \) ________

\( A, S, M \) and \( D \) are functions of \( x \) and \( y \), and they are defined as follows.

  • \( A(x, y) = x + y \)
  • \( M(x, y) = xy \)
  • \( S(x,y)= x-y \)
  • \( D(x,y)= x/y, y\neq 0 \)

What is the value of \( M(M(A(M(x, y), S(y, x)), x), A(y, x)) \) for \( x = 2, y = 3 \)?
